levelledPara.title: General
levelledPara.para: The ATEST aircraft is equipped with a tricycle retractable landing gear configuration consisting of:

listItem.para: Two main gear assemblies (left and right wing roots)

levelledPara.para: All gear legs retract into bays with flush-fitting doors operated by the hydraulic system. Gear extension and retraction uses hydraulic actuators supplied at 3000 PSI by the hydraulic power system (ATA 29).

levelledPara.title: Retraction System
levelledPara.para: The retraction cylinder assembly converts hydraulic pressure into linear motion to retract and extend the main gear leg. Each main gear has one retraction cylinder (P/N LG-CYL-MN-L for left, LG-CYL-MN-R for right).

levelledPara.title: Gear Door System
levelledPara.para: Each main gear bay has inboard and outboard doors. The inboard door opens first (powered by hydraulic actuator), followed by gear extension, then inboard door closes. The outboard door opens with the gear and closes after gear is down and locked.
Door position is monitored by proximity sensors feeding the gear position indication system in the cockpit.

levelledPara.title: Gear Position Indication
levelledPara.para: The cockpit gear position indicator shows three green lights (gear down and locked), three red lights (gear in transit or unsafe), or lights off (gear up and locked). The indicator is powered by the avionics bus.
A gear warning horn sounds when the throttle is reduced below approach power and gear is not down and locked.
